records.ety: [Icel. sæmiligr, fr. sæmr becoming, fit; akin to samr same, E. same; the sense being properly, the same or like, hence, fitting. See Seem, v. i.]

defs: Suited to the object, occasion, purpose, or character; suitable; fit; becoming; comely; decorous.
quotes: I am a woman, lacking wit To make a seemly answer to such persons.
Suspense of judgment and exercise of charity were safer and seemlier for Christian men than the hot pursuit of these controversies.

defs: In a decent or suitable manner; becomingly.
quotes: Suddenly a men before him stood, Not rustic as before, but seemlier clad, As one in city or court or place bred.
